title: Qualys VMDR XML Response Parsing — Detection List, Host List, Flatten to Rows
applies_to: Flattening host detections to tabular data

confirmed_facts.fact: Host Detection List XML structure: ROOT > RESPONSE > HOST_LIST > HOST > (ID, IP, TRACKING_METHOD, DNS, NETBIOS, OS, LAST_SCAN_DATETIME, DETECTION_LIST > DETECTION[])

DETECTION element fields: QID, TYPE (Confirmed/Potential/Info), SEVERITY (1-5), PORT, PROTOCOL, SSL, STATUS (New/Active/Fixed/Reopened), FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME, LAST_FOUND_DATETIME, TIMES_FOUND, RESULTS, QDS (when show_qds=1)
One HOST element contains MULTIPLE DETECTION elements — the data model is host-nested-detections, NOT flat rows
Host List XML structure (asset inventory): ROOT > RESPONSE > HOST_LIST > HOST > (ID, IP, TRACKING_METHOD, DNS, NETBIOS, OS, LAST_VULN_SCAN_DATETIME, TAGS > TAG[])
KnowledgeBase VULN element fields: QID, VULN_TYPE, SEVERITY_LEVEL, TITLE, CATEGORY, CVE_LIST > CVE > ID, CVSS > BASE (score), CVSS_V3 > BASE (score), SOLUTION, DIAGNOSIS
SEVERITY values: 1=Minimal, 2=Medium, 3=Serious, 4=Critical, 5=Urgent. This is the Qualys severity scale, NOT CVSS.
QDS (Qualys Detection Score) ranges 1-100. QDS severity mapping: 90-100=Critical, 70-89=High, 40-69=Medium, 1-39=Low
RESULTS field contains verbose scan output text (banner grabs, version strings, etc.) — it can be very large. Skip it for summary exports to save memory.

implementation_notes:
Parse host detection XML and flatten to rows:
```python
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def flatten_detections(xml_text: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Flatten nested HOST > DETECTION XML into one row per detection."""
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_text)
    rows = []
    for host in root.findall('.//HOST'):
        host_data = {
            'host_id': host.findtext('ID', ''),
            'ip': host.findtext('IP', ''),
            'dns': host.findtext('DNS', ''),
            'netbios': host.findtext('NETBIOS', ''),
            'os': host.findtext('OS', ''),
            'last_scan': host.findtext('LAST_SCAN_DATETIME', ''),
        }
        for det in host.findall('.//DETECTION'):
            row = {
                **host_data,
                'qid': det.findtext('QID', ''),
                'type': det.findtext('TYPE', ''),
                'severity': det.findtext('SEVERITY', ''),
                'port': det.findtext('PORT', ''),
                'protocol': det.findtext('PROTOCOL', ''),
                'ssl': det.findtext('SSL', ''),
                'status': det.findtext('STATUS', ''),
                'first_found': det.findtext('FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME', ''),
                'last_found': det.findtext('LAST_FOUND_DATETIME', ''),
                'times_found': det.findtext('TIMES_FOUND', ''),
                'qds': det.findtext('QDS', ''),
            }
            rows.append(row)
    return rows
```

Parse host detection XML from API response with truncation:
```python
def parse_detection_response(xml_text: str) -> tuple[list, bool, str | None]:
    """Returns (host_elements, is_truncated, next_id_min)."""
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_text)
    hosts = root.findall('.//HOST')
    
    warning = root.find('.//WARNING')
    is_truncated = False
    next_id_min = None
    if warning is not None:
        text = warning.findtext('TEXT', '')
        if 'Truncated' in text:
            is_truncated = True
            url = warning.findtext('URL', '')
            import re
            m = re.search(r'id_min=(\d+)', url)
            if m:
                next_id_min = m.group(1)
    
    return hosts, is_truncated, next_id_min
```

Enrich detections with KnowledgeBase data (add CVEs, titles):
```python
def enrich_with_kb(rows: list[dict], kb_map: dict) -> list[dict]:
    """Add KB fields (title, cves, cvss) to flattened detection rows."""
    for row in rows:
        qid = row.get('qid', '')
        vuln = kb_map.get(qid)
        if vuln is not None:
            row['title'] = vuln.findtext('TITLE', '')
            row['category'] = vuln.findtext('CATEGORY', '')
            cves = [cve.findtext('ID', '') for cve in vuln.findall('.//CVE_LIST/CVE')]
            row['cves'] = ', '.join(cves)
            row['cvss_base'] = vuln.findtext('.//CVSS/BASE', '')
            row['cvss3_base'] = vuln.findtext('.//CVSS_V3/BASE', '')
            row['solution'] = vuln.findtext('SOLUTION', '')
        else:
            row['title'] = ''
            row['cves'] = ''
            row['cvss_base'] = ''
            row['cvss3_base'] = ''
            row['solution'] = ''
    return rows
```

Convert to pandas DataFrame:
```python
import pandas as pd

def detections_to_dataframe(rows: list[dict]) -> pd.DataFrame:
    df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
    df['severity'] = pd.to_numeric(df['severity'], errors='coerce')
    df['qds'] = pd.to_numeric(df['qds'], errors='coerce')
    df['cvss_base'] = pd.to_numeric(df['cvss_base'], errors='coerce')
    df['cvss3_base'] = pd.to_numeric(df['cvss3_base'], errors='coerce')
    for col in ['first_found', 'last_found', 'last_scan']:
        df[col] = pd.to_datetime(df[col], errors='coerce', utc=True)
    return df
```

QDS severity label mapping:
```python
def qds_severity(qds: int | float) -> str:
    if qds >= 90: return 'Critical'
    if qds >= 70: return 'High'
    if qds >= 40: return 'Medium'
    return 'Low'
```
caveats: ET.fromstring() loads entire XML into memory — for very large exports (>500MB), use iterparse with incremental processing
RESULTS field can contain HTML entities and very long text — exclude it from flattening unless specifically needed
Not all detections have QDS — older detections or Info-type may have no QDS value. Handle as NaN/empty.
HOST ID is Qualys-internal — it is NOT the same as the asset ID from Global Asset Inventory
Some hosts have zero detections (empty DETECTION_LIST) — these appear in host-only queries but produce no rows when flattened
